threats.title: Service Identity Mimicry targeting [Webfront]
threats.desc: Malicious system processes spoof verification profiles or intercept routing instructions streams to inherit valid handshake states.

threats.title: DNS Cache Poisoning & Routing Redirection
threats.desc: Lack of secure DNS resolution constraints allows local infrastructure daemons to process forged names mappings, routing traffic to adversary pods.

threats.title: Storage Locking Starvation via Deadlock Query Insertion
threats.desc: Unbounded long-running queries or malicious read locks saturate connection allocation slots, rendering persistence layers unavailable.

threats.title: Wildcard Query Index Exhaustion and RAM Thrashing
threats.desc: Massive unindexed regular expression operations saturate persistence layer memory allocations, forcing server terminations.

threats.title: Credential Stuffing via Automated Brute-Force Replay Gates
threats.desc: Lack of client-side rate controls or device fingerprinting lets rogue bots masquerade as legitimate client instances using compiled credential lists.

threats.title: Client Session Hijacking and Cryptographic Claim Forgery
threats.desc: Weak cookie protection parameters or session token generation algorithms allow thread actors to steal active administrative claims.
